name: ogb-suite

env_axis.name: application-split
env_axis.shift_mechanism: 13+ sub-datasets with sub-dataset-specific split rules — curator-chosen, not naturally emergent

schema.suite_note: Suite — 13+ sub-datasets across node/link/graph property prediction. ogbn-arxiv broken out separately as a core member within the suite.

facts: 13+ sub-datasets across node/link/graph property prediction; application-specific splits; Hu et al. NeurIPS 2020.
hypotheses: The 'application-specific' splits produce consistent shift mechanisms within each shift-type: all year-splits behave similarly across sub-datasets.
A single cross-OGB method wins on temporal, scaffold, and species splits simultaneously.
intuitions: An OGB leaderboard SOTA for 2024-25 and graph-foundation-model attempts spanning multiple OGB sub-datasets likely exist.
A year-split critique literature probably exists, since ogbn-arxiv's boundary is debated.
unknowns: Whether OGB's splits are natural or curator-chosen cannot be determined — year-2017, a specific scaffold-bin, and a specific Wikidata-snapshot were all designer decisions, and there is no established label for 'split-designer as confounder'.
epistemic_state.boundary: Profile coverage is narrow — mostly (P=0, T=0-2, H=1-2, C=0-3, Ty=2-3); silent on process and most temporal dynamics.

representational_commitments: Application-specific split rules per sub-dataset (curator decisions)
Sub-dataset selection from larger source databases
MATERIALIZATION STATUS: ogbn-arxiv and ogb-mag fully materialized (separate manifests). ogbg-molhiv materialized (snapshots/ogb-suite/v1-2020/ogbg-molhiv.tar.gz). Other 10 sub-datasets pending.

curation_provenance.motivation: Realistic ML evaluation on graph data; standardize away random splits
